info.title: LegionIO REST API
info.description: Async job engine and digital worker platform REST API. All routes are under the /api/ prefix. Success responses wrap data in { data: ..., meta: { timestamp, node } }. Error responses use { error: { code, message }, meta: { timestamp, node } }.

tags.name: Events
tags.description: SSE event stream and recent event buffer

get.description: Returns ok status and version. Skips auth middleware.
Returns readiness status for all components. Returns 503 if not ready. Skips auth middleware.
Returns a paginated list of tasks ordered by id descending. Optionally filtered by status or function_id.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

post.description: Dispatches a task via Legion::Ingress. Requires runner_class and function. Additional keys in the request body are forwarded as the task payload. Returns 422 if runner_class or function are missing or invalid.

get.description: Returns a single task by id. Includes metering data (LLM token usage) when metering records exist for this task.

delete.description: Deletes a task record by id.

get.description: Returns all log entries for a task, ordered by id descending. Returns 404 if the task does not exist.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of log entries to return

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of log entries to skip

get.description: Returns all registered extensions ordered by id. Optionally filter to only active extensions via the active query parameter.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

get.description: Returns a single extension by id.
Returns all runners belonging to the given extension, ordered by id. Returns 404 if the extension does not exist.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

get.description: Returns a single runner by runner_id within the given extension. Returns 404 if either the extension or the runner does not exist.
Returns all functions belonging to the given runner, ordered by id. Returns 404 if the extension or runner does not exist.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

get.description: Returns a single function by function_id within the given extension and runner. Returns 404 if the extension, runner, or function does not exist.

404.description: Extension, runner, or function not found

post.description: Dispatches a task via Legion::Ingress using the runner namespace and function name resolved from the database. The request body is forwarded as the task payload. Returns 422 if the runner class is invalid.

404.description: Extension, runner, or function not found

get.description: Returns all registered nodes ordered by id. Optionally filter by active status or node status string.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

get.description: Returns a single node by id.
Returns all schedules ordered by id. Pass active=true to return only active schedules. Requires lex-scheduler to be loaded; returns 503 if not available.

parameters.name: active
parameters.description: When true, return only active schedules

post.description: Creates a new cron or interval schedule for a function. Either cron or interval must be provided (not both required, but at least one). Returns 422 if function_id is missing or neither cron nor interval is provided. Requires lex-scheduler to be loaded.

get.description: Returns a single schedule by id. Requires lex-scheduler.

put.description: Updates one or more fields on an existing schedule. All request body fields are optional; only provided fields are applied. Requires lex-scheduler.

delete.description: Deletes a schedule by id. Requires lex-scheduler.

get.description: Returns execution log entries for a schedule, ordered by id descending. Returns 404 if the schedule does not exist. Requires lex-scheduler.
Returns all task relationships ordered by id. Requires legion-data to be connected.

post.description: Creates a new task relationship record. Requires legion-data to be connected.

get.description: Returns a single relationship by id. Requires legion-data.

put.description: Updates one or more fields on an existing relationship. All request body fields are optional; only provided fields are applied. Requires legion-data.

delete.description: Deletes a relationship record by id. Requires legion-data.

get.description: Stub endpoint, not yet implemented. The Chain data model does not exist yet; this route always returns 501.

501.description: Not implemented — chain data model is not yet available

post.description: Stub endpoint, not yet implemented. The Chain data model does not exist yet; this route always returns 501. When implemented, name will be required.

501.description: Not implemented — chain data model is not yet available

get.description: Stub endpoint, not yet implemented. The Chain data model does not exist yet; this route always returns 501.

501.description: Not implemented — chain data model is not yet available

put.description: Stub endpoint, not yet implemented. The Chain data model does not exist yet; this route always returns 501.

501.description: Not implemented — chain data model is not yet available

delete.description: Stub endpoint, not yet implemented. The Chain data model does not exist yet; this route always returns 501.

501.description: Not implemented — chain data model is not yet available

get.description: Returns the full settings hash with sensitive keys redacted. Sensitive keys include: password, secret, token, key, cert, private_key, api_key.
Returns the value for a single top-level settings key. Nested hash values are redacted for sensitive keys. Returns 404 if the key does not exist.

put.description: Updates the runtime value for a top-level settings key. The sections `crypt` and `transport` are read-only and return 403. The request body must include a `value` field; missing value returns 422.

get.description:
Opens a Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream. The connection stays open and the server pushes each Legion event as it is emitted. Each frame has the form:

  event: <event_name>\n
  data: <JSON payload>\n
  \n

The JSON payload is the event hash with all keys as strings. The stream ends only when the client disconnects. Set `Cache-Control: no-cache` on your client to avoid proxy buffering.
Returns the most recent events from the in-process ring buffer (max 100 entries). Defaults to the last 25 events. Use the `count` query parameter to request more.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Number of recent events to return (capped at 100)

get.description: Returns the current RabbitMQ transport connection status including whether the session and channel are open, and the connector type.
Returns a list of registered AMQP exchange subclasses known to this node. Returns an empty array if Legion::Transport::Exchange is not defined.
Returns a list of registered AMQP queue subclasses known to this node. Returns an empty array if Legion::Transport::Queue is not defined.

post.description: Publishes a message to a named AMQP exchange with a routing key. Both `exchange` and `routing_key` are required; returns 422 if either is missing. The optional `payload` object is merged into the Dynamic message body. Returns 201 on success or 500 if the publish raises an error.

get.description: Returns a list of digital workers ordered by id. Optionally filtered by team, owner_msid, lifecycle_state, risk_tier, or health_status.

post.description: Registers a new digital worker. Requires name, extension_name, entra_app_id, and owner_msid. Returns 422 if any required field is missing.

get.description: Returns all workers currently pending approval.
Returns a single digital worker by worker_id.

delete.description: Retires the worker by transitioning its lifecycle state to 'retired'. Returns 422 if the transition is not valid from the current state.

patch.description: Transitions the worker to a new lifecycle state. Requires 'state' in the request body. Returns 403 if governance or authority requirements are not met. Returns 422 if the transition is not valid.

422.description: Invalid lifecycle transition or missing field

get.description: Returns the health snapshot for a worker including heartbeat time and node metrics.
Returns tasks associated with a worker, ordered by id descending.
Returns lifecycle events for a worker. Note: lifecycle event persistence is not yet implemented; this endpoint currently returns an empty events array.

note.example: lifecycle event persistence is not yet implemented

get.description: Returns cost metering data for a worker. Requires lex-metering to be installed; without it, total_cost will be null.
Returns value metrics summary and recent entries for a worker. Optionally filtered by 'since' timestamp (ISO 8601). Defaults to last 7 days.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Return value entries since this timestamp (ISO 8601)

get.description: Returns combined value and cost data for ROI calculation. Cost data requires lex-metering; without it, token/call counts will be zero.

post.description: Approves a worker pending registration. Transitions the worker to the approved/active lifecycle state. Returns 422 if the worker is not in a pending state.
Rejects a worker pending registration. Requires 'reason' in the request body. Returns 422 if the worker is not in a pending state or reason is missing.

422.description: Invalid request, missing reason, or invalid lifecycle transition

get.description: Returns all digital workers belonging to the specified team, ordered by id.
Returns aggregated cost metering data for all workers in a team. Requires lex-metering to be installed; without it, total_cost will be null.
Returns all extension webhook hooks registered with the Legion API. Each entry includes the extension name, hook name, hook class, default runner, and the trigger endpoint path.

Dispatches a registered extension hook using a GET request. The path parameter captures the full route_path registered by the extension (e.g. github/events). Returns 404 if no hook is registered for the given path. Returns 401 if hook verification fails. Returns 422 if the hook cannot route the event. Returns 500 if no runner class is configured. On success, returns the task_id and status from the dispatched Ingress run.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Hook route path registered by the extension

422.description: Hook could not route this event

500.description: No runner configured or internal error

post.description: Dispatches a registered extension hook using a POST request. The path parameter captures the full route_path registered by the extension (e.g. github/events). The request body is read as the hook payload and passed to the hook class for verification and routing. Returns 404 if no hook is registered for the given path. Returns 401 if hook verification fails. Returns 422 if the hook cannot route the event. Returns 500 if no runner class is configured. On success, returns the task_id and status from the dispatched Ingress run.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Hook route path registered by the extension

schema.description: Arbitrary hook payload; structure is hook-specific

422.description: Hook could not route this event

500.description: No runner configured or internal error

get.description: Returns all auto-registered LEX runner routes. Each entry maps a URL path to the extension, runner, and function that will handle POST requests to that path. Routes are registered automatically by Builders::Routes during extension autobuild.

post.description: Dispatches a payload to the LEX runner registered at the given path. The path parameter captures the full route_path for the runner function (e.g. github/sync/repositories). The request body is passed as the runner payload via Legion::Ingress. Returns 404 if no route is registered for the given path. On success, returns the task_id, status, and result (if available synchronously).

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Full route path for the target LEX runner function. Format is typically extension/runner/function.

schema.description: Arbitrary payload passed to the runner function. Structure is runner-specific. Empty body is valid; an empty hash is used.

result.description: Synchronous result if available; omitted when async

post.description:
Sends a message to the LLM and returns a response. The response shape depends on which routing path handles the request:
- **200** — Tier 0 pattern cache hit (Legion::MCP::TierRouter). Returns immediately
  with `response`, `tier: 0`, `latency_ms`, and `pattern_confidence`.

- **201** — Synchronous response from the gateway pipeline or a direct LLM call.
  Returns `response` and a `meta` object with model, token counts, and routing info.

- **202** — Async queued. Returned when the cache is available and the
  `X-Legion-Sync` header is not set to `true`. Returns a `request_id` and
  `poll_key` to check completion status via the cache.

Requires the LLM subsystem to be started. Returns 503 if unavailable.

parameters.name: X-Legion-Sync
parameters.description: Set to `true` to force a synchronous response even when the cache is available. Without this header the endpoint may return 202 and queue the request asynchronously.

200.description: Tier 0 pattern cache hit — response served without calling an LLM

201.description: Synchronous LLM response (gateway pipeline or direct call)

502.description: Gateway pipeline error or LLM provider error

post.summary: Exchange Entra token for Legion human token
post.description:
Implements RFC 8693 token exchange. Accepts a Microsoft Entra ID JWT as `subject_token`, verifies it via JWKS, maps claims to Legion RBAC roles, and returns a Legion-issued Bearer token valid for 8 hours.
Requires `legion-crypt` with JWKS support and `legion-rbac` with `EntraClaimsMapper`. The Entra `tenant_id` must be configured under `rbac.entra.tenant_id` in Legion settings.

401.description: Entra token is expired or invalid

501.description: JWKS validation or claims mapper not loaded

post.summary: Exchange Entra token for Legion worker token
post.description:
Machine-to-machine token exchange for digital workers. Accepts a Microsoft Entra ID JWT issued to the worker's app registration, verifies it via JWKS, looks up the registered `DigitalWorker` record by `appid`/`azp` claim, and returns a Legion-issued Bearer token valid for 1 hour.
The worker must exist in legion-data and be in `active` lifecycle state. Requires `legion-crypt` JWKS support and `legion-data` to be connected.

401.description: Entra token expired, invalid, or missing appid claim

403.description: Worker exists but is not in active lifecycle state

404.description: No worker registered for the given Entra app ID

get.description:
Redirects the browser to the Microsoft Entra ID authorization endpoint to begin the human OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow. A short-lived CSRF state JWT is embedded in the redirect. On success, Entra redirects back to `/api/auth/callback`.
Requires `rbac.entra.tenant_id`, `rbac.entra.client_id`, and `rbac.entra.redirect_uri` to be configured in Legion settings.

Location.description: Full Entra OAuth authorize URL with client_id, redirect_uri, state, and scope

500.description: Entra OAuth settings are not configured

get.description:
Receives the authorization code from Entra after the user authenticates. Validates the CSRF state token, exchanges the code for an id_token via the Entra token endpoint, verifies the id_token via JWKS, maps claims to Legion RBAC roles, and issues a Legion human token.
If the request `Accept` header includes `application/json`, the response is a JSON token payload. Otherwise the browser is redirected to `entra.success_redirect` (or `/api/health`) with the token appended as an `#access_token=` fragment.

parameters.name: error
parameters.description: OAuth error code returned by Entra on failure

200.description: Legion human token issued (JSON response when Accept includes application/json)

400.description: OAuth error, missing code, or invalid CSRF state

500.description: Entra OAuth settings are not configured

502.description: Code exchange with Entra token endpoint failed

get.description: Returns the SAML 2.0 Service Provider metadata XML document. Used by IdPs to configure trust with this Legion node. Only available when SAML is enabled (`auth.saml.enabled: true`) and `ruby-saml` gem is loaded.

503.description: SAML SP is not configured or ruby-saml gem is not loaded

get.description:
Redirects the browser to the IdP SSO URL to begin the SAML 2.0 authentication flow. On success, the IdP posts a SAMLResponse to `/api/auth/saml/acs`.
Requires `auth.saml.idp_sso_url` and `auth.saml.sp_entity_id` to be configured in Legion settings.

Location.description: IdP SSO URL with encoded AuthnRequest

500.description: SAML IdP SSO URL or SP entity ID not configured

503.description: SAML SP is not configured or ruby-saml gem is not loaded

post.description: Receives the SAMLResponse posted by the IdP after successful authentication, validates the assertion, extracts claims (nameid, email, display_name, groups), maps groups to Legion RBAC roles, and issues a Legion human token valid for 8 hours.

SAMLResponse.description: Base64-encoded SAML assertion from the IdP

503.description: SAML SP is not configured or ruby-saml gem is not loaded

get.description: Returns all registered RBAC roles with name, description, and cross_team flag. Returns 501 if legion-rbac is not installed.
Returns a single role by name, including its permissions and deny rules. Returns 404 if the role does not exist, 501 if legion-rbac is not installed.

post.description: Evaluates a permission check via Legion::Rbac::PolicyEngine without enforcing. Returns the policy evaluation result. Returns 501 if legion-rbac is not installed.

get.description: Returns all RBAC role assignments ordered by id. Optionally filtered by team, role, or principal. Returns 501 if legion-rbac is not installed, 503 if legion-data is not connected.

post.description: Assigns a role to a principal. Principal type defaults to human. Optionally scoped to a team and/or given an expiry. Returns 422 on validation failure, 501/503 if dependencies unavailable.

delete.description: Removes a role assignment by id. Returns 404 if the assignment does not exist, 501/503 if dependencies unavailable.

get.description: Returns all RBAC runner grants ordered by id. Optionally filtered by team. Returns 501 if legion-rbac is not installed, 503 if legion-data is not connected.

post.description: Grants a team access to runners matching a pattern with specified actions. Actions are stored as a comma-separated string. Returns 422 on validation failure, 501/503 if dependencies unavailable.

delete.description: Removes a runner grant by id. Returns 404 if the grant does not exist, 501/503 if dependencies unavailable.

get.description: Returns all cross-team RBAC grants ordered by id. Returns 501 if legion-rbac is not installed, 503 if legion-data is not connected.

post.description: Grants a source team the ability to invoke runners in a target team matching a pattern with specified actions. Optionally time-limited via expires_at. Returns 422 on validation failure, 501/503 if dependencies unavailable.

delete.description: Removes a cross-team grant by id. Returns 404 if the grant does not exist, 501/503 if dependencies unavailable.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of audit log entries ordered by id descending. Optionally filtered by event_type, principal_id, source, status, since, or until. Requires legion-data to be connected.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Return entries created at or after this timestamp

parameters.name: until
parameters.description: Return entries created at or before this timestamp

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

get.description: Runs tamper-evident hash chain verification via lex-audit. Returns verification result including whether the chain is intact and where it breaks if not. Returns 503 if lex-audit is not loaded.

503.description: lex-audit not loaded or legion-data not connected

get.description: Returns a list of pending approval requests via lex-audit ApprovalQueue. Optionally filtered by tenant_id and limited in count. Returns 503 if lex-audit is not available or legion-data is not connected.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

503.description: lex-audit not available or legion-data not connected

post.description: Submits a new approval request via lex-audit ApprovalQueue. Requires approval_type and requester_id. Returns 422 if either is missing. Returns 503 if lex-audit is not available or legion-data is not connected.

503.description: lex-audit not available or legion-data not connected

get.description: Returns a single approval request by id via lex-audit ApprovalQueue. Returns 404 if not found, 503 if lex-audit is not available.

503.description: lex-audit not available or legion-data not connected

put.description: Approves a pending approval request by id via lex-audit ApprovalQueue. Requires reviewer_id. Returns 422 if missing, 503 if dependencies unavailable.

503.description: lex-audit not available or legion-data not connected

put.description: Rejects a pending approval request by id via lex-audit ApprovalQueue. Requires reviewer_id. Returns 422 if missing, 503 if dependencies unavailable.

503.description: lex-audit not available or legion-data not connected

BearerAuth.description: Legion-issued JWT token (worker or human scope)

TaskMetering.description: Aggregated LLM token usage for this task. Present only when metering records exist.

check_subtask.description: Whether to check for an existing subtask before creating a new one

generate_task.description: Whether to persist a task record for this invocation

TaskCreatedObject.description: Task creation result returned by Ingress.

TaskCreatedResponse.description: Response from POST /api/tasks — task_id and status from Ingress

by.description: Identity performing the transition (MSID or service name)

authority_verified.description: Confirm that the caller has verified authority for this transition

FunctionInvokeInput.description: Payload forwarded to the runner function. All extra keys are passed through as the task payload. check_subtask and generate_task control Ingress behavior.

task_ttl.description: Time-to-live for generated tasks, in seconds

payload.description: Arbitrary JSON payload forwarded to the runner on each execution.

transformation.description: Optional lex-transformer definition name to apply before execution

function_id.description: ID of the function to invoke on each tick

cron.description: Cron expression. Required when interval is not provided.

interval.description: Interval in seconds. Required when cron is not provided.

task_ttl.description: Time-to-live for generated tasks, in seconds

payload.description: Payload forwarded to the runner on each execution. Serialized to JSON when stored.

transformation.description: lex-transformer definition name to apply before execution

ScheduleUpdateInput.description: All fields are optional. Only provided fields are updated. Must include at least one of cron or interval if changing the trigger.

parent_id.description: ID of the parent task in the relationship

child_id.description: ID of the child task in the relationship

function_id.description: Function to invoke when the parent task completes

conditioner_id.description: Optional conditioner function applied before dispatching the child task

transformer_id.description: Optional transformer applied to the payload before dispatching

function_id.description: Function to invoke when the parent task completes

RelationshipUpdateInput.description: All fields are optional. Only provided fields are updated.

ChainObject.description: A named chain groups a sequence of task relationships into a reusable workflow. The Chain data model is not yet implemented; all chain endpoints return 501.

name.description: Unique human-readable name for the chain

description.description: Optional description of the chain's purpose
description.example: End-to-end invoice ingestion and approval workflow
End-to-end invoice ingestion and approval workflow

ChainUpdateInput.description: All fields are optional. Only provided fields are updated.

SettingObject.description: A single top-level settings key and its (possibly redacted) value

value.description: Settings value; may be a scalar, array, or nested object. Sensitive keys are redacted.

SettingUpdateInput.description: Request body for updating a settings key

value.description: New value for the settings key (scalar, array, or object)

SettingsResponse.description: Full settings hash with sensitive keys redacted

data.description: All top-level settings keys, sensitive values replaced with [REDACTED]

EventObject.description: A Legion in-process event captured in the ring buffer

node.description: Node identifier that emitted the event

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the event was emitted

connected.description: Whether the transport setting reports connected

session_open.description: Whether the AMQP session is open

channel_open.description: Whether the AMQP channel is open

PublishInput.description: Parameters for publishing a message to RabbitMQ

exchange.description: Name of the AMQP exchange to publish to

payload.description: Optional message payload merged into the Dynamic message body

message.description: The prompt or message to send to the LLM
message.example: What is the capital of France?

provider.description: Optional provider override (e.g. claude, openai, bedrock, gemini)

request_id.description: Optional idempotency key; server generates one if omitted

model.description: Model name used to generate the response

tier.description: Always 0 for Tier 0 pattern cache hits

latency_ms.description: Time in milliseconds to serve the response from cache

pattern_confidence.description: Confidence score of the matched pattern

response.example: Paris is the capital of France.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for the queued request

poll_key.description: Cache key to poll for completion status

grant_type.description: Must be the RFC 8693 token exchange grant type

subject_token.description: A valid Microsoft Entra ID JWT to exchange for a Legion token

entra_token.description: A valid Microsoft Entra ID JWT issued to the worker app registration

access_token.description: Legion-issued JWT for the authenticated human

roles.description: RBAC roles assigned to the user

team.description: Team the user belongs to (present on token exchange, may be null)

name.description: Display name of the authenticated user (present on OAuth callback and SAML ACS)

access_token.description: Legion-issued JWT for the digital worker

data.description: Map of role name to role summary object

AuditVerifyResult.description: Result of tamper-evident hash chain verification from lex-audit

broken_at.description: ID of first entry where chain is broken (null if valid)
